sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:

Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Level 1 has two white chambers separated by a purple vertical bar. The left chamber has a charcoal frame and a green frame with white center. Between chambers is a passage with a green frame (black center) and a white 3x3 square. The right chamber has a charcoal frame. The white 3x3 square at [30-32, 30-32] appears to be the player avatar in the passage area.
- Goal model: Navigate the player through the passage to the right chamber.
- Action model: UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT likely move the white square avatar.
- Plan: Try moving RIGHT again to continue through the passage toward the right chamber.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 3 of about 28 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the images, I can see that after the 3rd RIGHT move:
- The green frame with white center is now on the LEFT side (separated from the black-centered one)
- The green frame with black center is now in the RIGHT chamber (past the purple wall)
So it seems like the player's green frame split from the black-centered one, and the black-centered one moved through the passage into the right chamber. The white-centered green frame stayed behind.
Let me look at the current segmentation to understand what's happening. The green frame with black center seems to have passed through the purple wall into the right chamber. This might be the goal - to get the black token through the passage.
But wait, the level isn't complete yet. Maybe I need to get the white-centered frame through too, or maybe the black-centered one needs to reach a specific location in the right chamber.
Let me check the current state more carefully.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state - what green frames exist and where
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green_frames = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print("Green frames:")
for g in green_frames:
    print(f"  id={g['id']}, boundary={g['boundary']}, children={g['children']}")
    for cid in g['children']:
        child = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == cid][0]
        print(f"    child: color={child['color']}, boundary={child['boundary']}")

# Check charcoal frames
charcoal_frames = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'c']
print("\nCharcoal frames:")
for c in charcoal_frames:
    print(f"  id={c['id']}, boundary={c['boundary']}, children={c['children']}")
    for cid in c['children']:
        child = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == cid][0]
        print(f"    child: color={child['color']}, boundary={child['boundary']}")

# Check purple bar
purple = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'P']
print("\nPurple bar:")
for p in purple:
    print(f"  boundary={p['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:

1. Green frame with WHITE center at [30,24]-[32,26] - this is the player, still in the left chamber area
2. Green frame with BLACK center at [30,42]-[32,44] - this is in the RIGHT chamber, past the purple wall
3. Charcoal frame in right chamber at [26,44]-[30,48] with white interior
4. Charcoal frame in left chamber at [32,11]-[36,15] with white interior
5. No purple bar detected (it might be part of the background or a different color)
So the black-centered green frame has moved into the right chamber. The player (white-centered green frame) is still at [30,24]-[32,26]. The level isn't complete yet.
Looking at the image, I can see the green frame with black center is now in the right chamber, below the charcoal frame there. The player's green frame with white center is still in the passage area.
The goal might be to get the player (white-centered green frame) through the passage to the right chamber. The black-centered frame seems to have already passed through.
Let me try moving RIGHT again to get the player through the passage.

sections.content:

Plan: Continue moving RIGHT to get the player through the passage.

sections.content:
